plan.title: fix(templates,skills,tests): templates/swarm-greptile-poller-prompt.md \Last reviewed commit:\ regex breaks on markdown-escaped brackets in link text (PR #1267 recurrence)

narratives.Description: fix(templates,skills,tests): templates/swarm-greptile-poller-prompt.md \Last reviewed commit:\ regex breaks on markdown-escaped brackets in link text (PR #1267 recurrence)
narratives.Overview:
## Summary

The canonical Greptile poller template at `templates/swarm-greptile-poller-prompt.md` has a regex bug in the `Last reviewed commit:` SHA-extraction pattern. The current regex uses `[^\]]*` for the markdown-link text segment, which stops at the FIRST `]` in the body. When Greptile's rolling-summary link text contains markdown-escaped brackets (e.g. a commit title like `docs(changelog): add \[Unreleased\] entry ...`), the regex stops on the escaped `\]` and never reaches the actual link-closing `](`. Result: `last_reviewed_sha = None`, the CLEAN gate's first condition (`sha_match`) blocks, and the poller either fires a STALL (#1039) within ~4.5 min OR (pre-#1039) burns its 30-min TIMEOUT cap on a textbook-clean review.

## Recurrence record

PR #1267 (2026-05-22). The maintainer pushed two follow-up commits (merge from master + a CHANGELOG entry with title `docs(changelog): add [Unreleased] entry for #1267 ...`). The dispatched review-cycle sub-agent (`019e5151-5f5f-7c44-a8dc-ac1587450ffd`) wedged on the `sha_match` holdout for 3 consecutive polls and exited via STALL.

Ground truth on the PR: `Confidence Score: 5/5 -- Safe to merge`, 0 P0, 0 P1 across all four detector tiers, all 7 CI checks pass — the poller saw a clean review but could not parse the `Last reviewed commit:` link because the link text contained `\[Unreleased\]`. The (5) STALL exit fired correctly within ~3 min (the parse-gap fail-loud surface from #1039 worked as designed).

## Current regex (broken on escaped brackets)

```python
r"Last reviewed commit:\s*\[[^\]]*\]\(https?://github\.com/[^/]+/[^/]+/commit/(?P<sha>[0-9a-f]{7,40})"
```

`[^\]]*` is greedy negated-class and stops at the first `]`. In Greptile's rolling-summary link, the link text often includes the commit title verbatim — any commit subject containing literal `]` (markdown-escaped or otherwise) breaks the match.

## Suggested fix

Swap `[^\]]*` for non-greedy `.*?`:

```python
r"Last reviewed commit:\s*\[.*?\]\(https?://github\.com/[^/]+/[^/]+/commit/(?P<sha>[0-9a-f]{7,40})"
```

The non-greedy `.*?` lets the link text contain any character including escaped brackets while still stopping at the actual `](` that introduces the URL. Confirmed against Greptile's rolling-summary on PR #1267 — matches correctly.

## Surfaces to update

This is a multi-surface fix because the canonical regex is encoded in three places:

1. **`templates/swarm-greptile-poller-prompt.md`** — the primary canonical encoding (in both the inline regex example AND the `## Implementation Notes` section that cites the pattern verbatim).
2. **`skills/deft-directive-review-cycle/SKILL.md`** — the canonical SHA-comparison parse step (currently references the same broken regex shape inline).
3. **`tests/content/test_swarm_poller_template.py`** — the content sync tests that pin the canonical regex string. The replacement string MUST be updated in lockstep AND a new positive regression test added with a fixture body containing escaped brackets in the link text (e.g. `Last reviewed commit: [docs: add \[Unreleased\] entry ...](https://github.com/...)`).

## Acceptance criteria

- `templates/swarm-greptile-poller-prompt.md` `Last reviewed commit:` regex updated to use non-greedy `.*?` for link text.
- `skills/deft-directive-review-cycle/SKILL.md` mirror updated.
- `tests/content/test_swarm_poller_template.py` updated:
  - Sync test (pins the new canonical string) updated.
  - New positive regression test: synthetic Greptile body containing `[<title with \[Unreleased\]>](https://github.com/.../commit/<40-char-sha>)` → `last_reviewed_sha` extracted correctly.
  - Existing positive tests (link text without escaped brackets) continue to pass.
- A short cross-reference in `meta/lessons.md` `## Triple-Tier Greptile Findings Detector in Poller Template (2026-05)` (or a new sibling section) citing this issue + the PR #1267 recurrence record. Per `main.md` Rule Authority [AXIOM], the rule body lives in the template + the content test; the lessons entry is a cross-reference only.
- The new regression test fails on the broken regex AND passes on the proposed fix.

## Out of scope

- Other detector tiers (Tier 1 badge, Tier 2 bold-bullet, Tier 2.5 SLizard heading, Tier 3 inline-prose) are unaffected — they don't depend on link-text parsing.
- The `## Confidence parse` regex is also unaffected — link text isn't involved.
- The CLEAN-gate `evaluate_clean_gate` function is unaffected — the bug is purely upstream in the SHA-extraction step that feeds `last_reviewed_sha`.

## Recurrence chain

This is the fourth recurrence in the canonical poller template's detector-gap chain after:
- #910 (triple-tier detector — added Tier 2 and Tier 3 after badge-only false-negatives)
- #1035 (Tier 2.5 SLizard heading form + confidence-heading fallback)
- #1039 (5) STALL terminal exit + Tier 1 instrumentation + Tier 3 per-condition fail-loud — caught this case correctly via STALL within ~3 min instead of burning the 30-min TIMEOUT)

The #1039 STALL surface worked exactly as designed here. This issue closes the upstream parse-gap that triggered the STALL.
